notes: v1.13.8 — TRUE full-bleed (kill the top & bottom black bands). Follow-up to v1.13.7: the flex stage filled the column BETWEEN #boot's paddings, but #boot itself had padding: calc(safe-top+32px) 24px calc(safe-bottom+24px) -- that top/bottom padding WAS the dead band, and the top double-counted the safe-area (because .pl-hdr also adds calc(safe-top+24px)). Fix: #boot padding -> 0 24px 0 (top/bottom now edge-to-edge; .pl-hdr's own safe-top inset still clears the notch, no more double-count), and .pl-hint bottom -> calc(12px + var(--safe-bottom)) so the hint clears the home indicator while the globe background reaches the true bottom edge. Sides kept at 24px (the complaint was top/bottom; full-width is available on request). NOTE: this edits #boot, which the v1.13.7 task had scoped out -- but #boot's padding was the actual band, so the fix had to live there. Behavior unchanged (spin-to-online -> warp, UFO, space layer, inputs, reduced-motion, no-WebGL). Verified: node --check 3/3 inline scripts, CSS braces balanced, CRLF preserved on dct-ios.html & sw.js (0 lone LF; version.json stays LF), stamps lockstep at v1.13.8, sw.js cache-bumped. NOT device-verified -- needs an on-device look on iPhone Safari (notch + home-indicator clearance, no remaining bands).
